path.aria-label: Close one session before opening the next on heavy workdays.
Your closure deﬁcit clears 0.55 on the four spike days of the year — you spent more than
half those work hours juggling overlapping Claude sessions. CODL peaks stayed within working-memory capacity (Cowan 2001), so the load is from
leaving sessions open, not from running too many in parallel.
Several Saturdays this year logged 2+ hours of off-hours activity. Detachment failure on weekends compounds the work-week
load rather than relieving it (Sonnentag 2010); even one heavy Saturday erodes recovery for the days around it.
Monthly cells show the average composite score over the month's active workdays. The line below shows each workday as a bar colored by its composite score (same palette as the calendar heatmap). The
dashed green line marks your typical-day composite (p50) — a health reference.
Daily composite stress · low to high
> 0 min of activity outside detected work window on 57 day(s)
Psychological detachment from work in off-hours predicts next-day vigor and longer-term well-being; off-hours engagement after a heavy week compounds
Staying well and engaged when demands are high
Parallel-stream fan-out exceeded WM capacity on 10 days
peak concurrent streams ≥ 4 on 10 workdays (max observed: 5)
Supervisory-control studies identify fan-out limits where performance degrades non-linearly and subjective stress rises. Sequencing two streams often produces
equal throughput with materially lower cognitive cost.
Predicting Controller Capacity in Supervisory Control of Multiple UAVs
These signals are behavioral proxies for cognitive load and recovery, not a clinical assessment. For burnout evaluation, the Maslach Burnout Inventory (Maslach &
optimum 1.2 CODL · aim to stay near
